data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260511T001956-23ed1d5c/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260511T001956-23ed1d5c/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260511T001956-23ed1d5c/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260511T001956-23ed1d5c/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260511T001956-23ed1d5c/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-a9ff01a1">
    <title>fix(review): reviewer ratifies ac-check + REQUEST_CLARIFICATION verdict + strictness by kind:</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  Sister bead (ddx-c739d7ec) introduces `ddx bead ac-check` —
  a mechanical per-AC verification tool that produces
  `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json`. This bead
  changes the reviewer behavior to:
    1. Invoke ac-check during pre-commit so implementer can
       self-check before submitting (closes information
       asymmetry).
    2. Pass ac-check.json into the reviewer prompt as
       structured input. Reviewer ratifies or overrides;
       does not re-grep the diff independently.
    3. Add REQUEST_CLARIFICATION verdict for prose AC items
       — does NOT BLOCK the queue; pings operator.
    4. Strictness modes driven by bead labels: kind:fix /
       kind:feat = strict; kind:refactor / kind:chore =
       behavior-light; kind:doc / kind:mechanical = file
       evidence only.

  Without this bead, ac-check is descriptive only —
  implementer can ignore it, reviewer doesn't consume it.

CURRENT STATE
  - Sister bead ddx-c739d7ec lands the ac-check tool but does
    not wire it into the implementer or reviewer flow.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:204-234 emits
    per_ac[].grade + per_ac[].evidence with no structured
    input — reviewer greps raw diff.
  - Reviewer verdicts: APPROVE, BLOCK. No REQUEST_CLARIFICATION.
  - Reviewer strictness is uniform — no per-kind tuning.

PROPOSED FIX
  Three coordinated changes:

  1. Wire ac-check into execute-bead implementer flow:
     - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (or execute_bead_loop.go):
       after staging changes and BEFORE creating the
       implementer's commit, invoke `ddx bead ac-check <id>`.
     - Capture the ac-check.json into the attempt's evidence
       dir.
     - The implementer commit's body must include an
       `AC-Check:` trailer summarizing the result (e.g.,
       `AC-Check: 3 pass / 1 fail / 1 needs-judgment`).
     - If `fail` count > 0 without an explicit `AC-Waive: #N
       <reason>` trailer in the commit body, abort the attempt
       and route through the existing escalation path.

  2. Update reviewer prompt to consume ac-check.json:
     - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go
       beadReviewInstructions (or the canonical prompt
       template): inject ac-check.json as a `<ac-check>`
       section.
     - Reviewer's job becomes: ratify the mechanical results
       per AC, OR override with explicit
       `judgment_override.reason`. Override requires concrete
       diff citation.
     - Reviewer adjudicates `needs-judgment` items
       (judgment-only AC) and chooses pass/fail/clarify.
     - New verdict: REQUEST_CLARIFICATION. Used when the
       reviewer cannot adjudicate a needs-judgment item
       without more bead context. Routes to the operator
       lane (status=proposed with explanatory label) — does
       NOT block the drain queue further.

  3. Strictness modes by bead label:
     - kind:fix, kind:feat → strict (default; current behavior).
     - kind:refactor, kind:chore → behavior-light: pass = build
       green + symbol/file evidence; do not require test-name
       match unless AC explicitly names a Test*.
     - kind:doc, kind:mechanical → mechanical: file/rename/
       symbol evidence only.
     - Reviewer reads b.Labels and applies the mode by
       picking different prompt-section templates.

  Tests:
     - TestReviewer_RatifiesACCheck_AllPass_ApproveImplicit
     - TestReviewer_RatifiesACCheck_Fail_BlocksByRatification
     - TestReviewer_NeedsJudgment_AdjudicatedOrClarification
     - TestReviewer_RequestClarification_NotBlockingQueue
     - TestReviewer_OverridePassToBlock_RequiresJudgmentReason
     - TestReviewer_StrictnessMode_KindFix_RequiresTestNames
     - TestReviewer_StrictnessMode_KindRefactor_BuildGreenSuffices
     - TestReviewer_StrictnessMode_KindDoc_FilePresenceSuffices
     - TestImplementerFlow_ACCheckFail_AbortsWithoutWaive
     - TestImplementerFlow_ACWaiveTrailer_PassesAttempt
     - TestReviewer_RegressionFakeRetainDays_BlocksByACCheck

NON-SCOPE
  - The ac-check tool itself (sister bead ddx-c739d7ec).
  - Implementer prompt changes (out of scope; the AC-Check
    trailer requirement is added at the orchestrator level).
  - Manual operator override tools.

INTERSECTIONS
  - DEPENDS ON: sister bead ddx-c739d7ec (ac-check tool) must
    land first.
  - Sister bead ddx-451cdead (ADR-024 amendment) — P3 reviewer
    asymmetry principle aligns with the strictness-by-kind
    mode here.
  - The three observed fake-work cases (ddx-1f774edf,
    ddx-94a368b6, ddx-d22293ba) should retroactively BLOCK
    under the new flow because their ac-check would fail.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (or sibling) invokes `ddx bead ac-check <id>` after staging and before commit; output captured to .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json.
2. Implementer's commit body contains an `AC-Check:` trailer summarizing the result; `fail` count > 0 without an `AC-Waive:` trailer aborts the attempt.
3. Reviewer prompt (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go or canonical template) consumes ac-check.json as structured input.
4. Reviewer ratifies mechanical results; override requires explicit `judgment_override.reason` with diff citation.
5. New verdict REQUEST_CLARIFICATION exists; reviewer uses it for needs-judgment items it cannot adjudicate; routes to operator lane (status=proposed with label) without blocking the queue.
6. Reviewer applies strictness mode by bead label: kind:fix/feat=strict, kind:refactor/chore=behavior-light, kind:doc/mechanical=file-evidence-only.
7. TestReviewer_RatifiesACCheck_AllPass_ApproveImplicit, TestReviewer_RatifiesACCheck_Fail_BlocksByRatification, TestReviewer_NeedsJudgment_AdjudicatedOrClarification, TestReviewer_RequestClarification_NotBlockingQueue, TestReviewer_OverridePassToBlock_RequiresJudgmentReason, TestReviewer_StrictnessMode_KindFix_RequiresTestNames, TestReviewer_StrictnessMode_KindRefactor_BuildGreenSuffices, TestReviewer_StrictnessMode_KindDoc_FilePresenceSuffices, TestImplementerFlow_ACCheckFail_AbortsWithoutWaive, TestImplementerFlow_ACWaiveTrailer_PassesAttempt, TestReviewer_RegressionFakeRetainDays_BlocksByACCheck all pass.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, area:review, kind:fix, observed-failure, prevention, spec:ADR-024</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="612502d4c9218a8b0adf280635729ae61dd49611" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260511T001956-23ed1d5c"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
The commit landed cleanly. The implementation is complete:

- **`bead/park.go`**: Added `ParkReviewRequestClarification` reason + metadata map entry
- **`execute_bead_status.go`**: Added `ExecuteBeadStatusACCheckFailed` status constant
- **`review_verdict.go`**: Added `VerdictRequestClarification` and updated `ParseReviewVerdict` to accept it
- **`execute_bead_review.go`**: New `beadReviewInstructions` with strictness mode/AC-check ratification/REQUEST_CLARIFICATION; `beadStrictnessMode()` helper; `ACCheckJSON` field in `BuildReviewPromptOptions`; `<ac-check>` section injection; `readACCheckJSON()` helper; `reviewBeadWithDiff` reads ac-check.json via `impl.Correlation["attempt_id"]`
- **`execute_bead_review_terminal.go`**: `ReviewRequestClarificationEventKind` + `applyReviewRequestClarification()` (parks via `ParkReviewRequestClarification`, appends event)
- **`execute_bead_post_review.go`**: `VerdictRequestClarification` case in the verdict switch
- **`execute_bead_loop.go`**: `isValidImplementationAttempt` excludes the two new status codes
- **`execute_bead.go`**: `ACCheckRunner` field on `ExecuteBeadRuntime`; pre-commit gate writes `ac-check.json`, appends `AC-Check:` trailer, aborts on unwaived fails; `allFailsWaived`/`readWaiveSet`/`buildACWaiveTrailers` helpers
- **`execute_bead_review_test.go`**: All 11 named tests pass
- **`store_test.go`**: Updated `TestParkToProposed` to include the new park reason
- **`collapse_golden.txt`**: Regenerated to match new review instructions
